assertions.assertion: Whale wallet identification must use quantifiable thresholds based on network-wide distribution

do: Define whale as address holding >10,000 ETH or equivalent value
Use top 0.1% of addresses by balance as whale threshold
Track addresses with >$10M in stablecoin holdings
dont: Use arbitrary thresholds without statistical justification
Ignore address clustering and entity resolution
Track retail-sized wallets (< $1M) as whale activity
Fail to distinguish between exchange hot wallets and actual whales

assertions.commentary: Whale definition must be statistically meaningful. Using network-wide distribution percentiles ensures focus on truly significant market participants, not noise from mid-size holders.

assertions.assertion: Exchange flows must distinguish between deposit (potential sell pressure) and withdrawal (potential accumulation) patterns

do: Track net exchange flow (inflow minus outflow) over 24h window
Identify large single deposits to exchanges (>1000 ETH)
Monitor sustained withdrawal patterns from exchanges
Correlate exchange flows with price movements
dont: Report total exchange volume without directional breakdown
Ignore timing correlation with price action
Treat all exchange movements as equivalent signals
Fail to distinguish between exchange-to-exchange transfers and genuine deposits/withdrawals

assertions.commentary: Directional flow matters critically. Large exchange deposits often precede selling, while sustained withdrawals suggest accumulation. Context and direction determine signal quality.

assertions.assertion: Smart money positioning must be tracked through DeFi protocol interactions, not just raw transfers

do: Monitor whale interactions with lending protocols (collateral changes)
Track whale LP positions in major DEXs
Identify whale participation in governance votes
dont: Ignore DeFi positioning changes that signal market views
Miss whale rotation between asset classes

assertions.commentary: Sophisticated whales express market views through DeFi actions, not just transfers. Protocol interactions reveal positioning, leverage, and risk appetite more clearly than simple wallet-to-wallet movements.

assertions.assertion: Analysis must provide clear implications for market direction and potential trade setups

do: Connect whale accumulation to bullish setup hypothesis
Link sustained exchange deposits to potential resistance levels
Identify whale capitulation patterns as potential reversal signals
Quantify confluence of multiple whale signals
dont: Present whale movements without market direction implications
Fail to distinguish between bullish and bearish positioning
Report activity without connecting to actionable trade ideas
Ignore conflicting signals from different whale cohorts

assertions.commentary: Whale tracking value lies in predictive power. Analysis must translate observed positioning into clear market direction hypotheses and specific trade entry/exit considerations.
